name: Under Armour, Inc.

profile.description: Under Armour is a global athletic apparel and footwear brand. 6,200 employees. Revenue declined 0.8% YoY. Brand turnaround underway under returning founder Kevin Plank.

innovation.label: WEAK
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Revenue declined 0.8% reflecting brand challenges. AI Deployment (0/2): No AI products. Market Share (0/2): Losing share to Nike, Adidas, Hoka, and On Running. Product Pipeline (1/2): Brand repositioning under founder Kevin Plank. R&D Intensity (1/1): Athletic technology R&D.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): 'Make all athletes better' is specific and has shaped product innovation. Community Impact (0/2): No specific programs. Employee Retention (0/2): 6,200 after layoffs. Insider Buying (1/2): Founder Plank maintains significant stake.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Revenue declined 0.8% with -9.98% margin and -30% ROE [1]. D/E of 137.14% elevated. Forward P/E of 23.03 and PEG of 1.84 reflect recovery expectations.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Physical apparel manufacturing and retail distribution are largely immune to AI. Brand building has some AI enhancement. AI Opportunity (0/1): No AI revenue.

infinite_game.summary: Under Armour under returning founder Kevin Plank has a genuine Just Cause in athletic performance, but the company's recent struggles, leadership turnover, and brand erosion suggest finite game pressures.

investment_thesis: Under Armour's brand reset under founder Kevin Plank at $5.58 offers recovery optionality [1]. 'Make all athletes better' mission provides product development direction.
thesis_continued: Revenue declined 0.8% with -9.98% margin and -30% ROE [1]. Brand losing share to Nike, Hoka, On Running. D/E of 137.14% limits flexibility.
key_catalysts: Brand repositioning under founder Kevin Plank driving product innovation
Athletic footwear market share gains in performance categories
International market expansion, particularly in Asia
key_risks: Brand erosion continuing as Nike, Hoka, and On Running gain market share
Revenue decline extending with -0.8% growth trajectory
High D/E of 137.14% limiting strategic flexibility during turnaround
sources: Yahoo Finance — UAA Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
